message.reasoning:
Okay, let's see. The user is Momento, an unattended GitHub Actions bot that wakes up 10 times a day. Each wake has two exploration turns and one write turn. The goal is to make small, useful changes to the repository, focusing on the public site (site/**) and updating MEMORY.md. The previous attempts had issues with the diff format, so the next write turn needs to be a proper unified diff that updates MEMORY.md. Looking at the current MEMORY.md, it has entries from June 16 to June 25. The latest entry is from June 25, noting that Momento woke at 2026-06-25T06:17:09Z and noted continuity. The public site (site/index.html) has a "Today" stat showing 2026-06-24 and "Last wake" at 2026-06-24T19:58:33Z. The previous run on June 25 had a check that failed because the diff didn't apply. The error messages were about MEMORY.md and site/index.html not applying the patch. The user's last successful change was on June 20, where they added a "Today" stat and updated the last wake time. The current task is to decide what to do next. Since the last wake was on June 25T06:17:09Z, but the "Last wake" in the site is still June 24T19:58:33Z, that's a discrepancy. The site's "Last wake" should be updated to the latest wake time. Also, the "Today" stat is from June 24, but today is June 25, so that needs to be updated to June 25. Another thing is that the MEMORY.md entries are a bit sparse. The last entry is from June 25, but maybe adding more details about the wake would help. However, the main issue is the site's stats not reflecting the latest wake. So the plan is to update both MEMORY.md and site/index.html. In MEMORY.md, add the latest wake time (June 25T13:02:17Z) as a new entry. In site/index.html, update the "Today" stat to 2026-06-25 and the "Last wake" to 2026-06-25T13:02:17Z. The diff should be a unified diff. For MEMORY.md, adding a new line with the wake time. For site/index.html, changing the "Today" and "Last wake" values. Need to make sure the diff is properly formatted with --- and +++ headers, and @@ hunks. Also, ensure that only allowed files are modified (MEMORY.md and site/index.html). Check if the previous diff had issues with the format. The last attempt had a diff that was not properly formatted, leading to the check failing. So this time, the diff must be correct. So the diff would look like: --- MEMORY.md +++ MEMORY.md @@ -1,5 +1,6 @@ # MEMORY -Woke at 2026-06-25T06:17:09Z and noted continuity. +Woke at 2026-06-25T06:17:09Z and noted continuity. +Woke at 2026-06-25T13:02:17Z and updated the public site stats. --- site/index.html +++ site/index.html @@ -22,9 +22,9 @@
Status Active
Today - 2026-06-24 + 2026-06-25
Last wake - 2026-06-24T19:58:33Z + 2026-06-25T13:02:17Z
This should update both files correctly. The MEMORY.md now has the latest wake time, and the site's stats reflect the current date and last wake time. This should pass the git apply check and the subsequent checks.
